decisions.decision: Keep this step as a narrow additive change in `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts` by adding only a happy-path `getAccountExposure(accountId)` regression beside the existing wrapper tests, without expanding scope into exposure-specific `BayesApiError` assertions.
alternatives_rejected: Add new exposure-specific non-OK coverage in the same step.
Create a separate test file or broader client-test reorganization for one wrapper assertion.

decisions.decision: Freeze the full backend-shaped `AccountExposureResponse` envelope in the new mock response and result assertion, including `account.id`, `exposure.maxPositionSize`, `exposure.updatedAt`, every `positions[]` field, and `meta`, so the frontend client test doubles as a contract check against the existing serializer/types.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only a representative subset of the exposure payload after checking the path.
Use an arbitrary frontend-only fixture that is merely TypeScript-valid.

decisions.decision: Follow the existing simple wrapper-test style and assert the plain request path `/v1/accounts/a1/exposure`, leaving explicit path-encoding coverage out of this step even though the production client uses `encodeURIComponent(accountId)`.
alternatives_rejected: Use an account ID that requires escaping to freeze encoding behavior in this same regression.

steps.task: Inspect `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts`, `frontend/src/lib/api/client.ts`, and `frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts` together so the new case imports `getAccountExposure` if needed, matches the existing mocked-fetch style, and uses the exact `AccountExposureResponse` field set already exposed by the client/types.

Extend `frontend/src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts` with one `getAccountExposure("a1")` success test that resolves `mockFetch` with a backend-derived exposure fixture, asserts `fetch` was called with `/v1/accounts/a1/exposure`, and verifies the returned value preserves the full frozen response envelope consumed by the frontend.

Run the targeted frontend regression command `pnpm --dir frontend test -- src/__tests__/api-client.test.ts` (or the equivalent file-scoped Vitest invocation accepted by the script) and confirm the new wrapper coverage passes without widening unrelated scope.
